seo.title: Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix prompt injection prevention for ai agents in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, prompt injection in AI agents manifests as attackers exploiting the multi-agent conversation flow, particularly through group chat speaker selection and message passing. Malicious inputs can hijack agent roles, manipulate consensus mechanisms, or trigger harmful actions in code execution agents. AG2's enhanced group chat capabilities amplify these risks as injected prompts propagate across agent conversations, leading to cascading failures.

howItManifests.scenario: Malicious user message in group chat
howItManifests.impact: Group consensus manipulated to approve harmful actions

howItManifests.scenario: Injected code in code execution agent input
howItManifests.symptom: Agent conversation spirals into executing dangerous scripts
howItManifests.impact: System compromise through arbitrary code execution

howItManifests.scenario: Cross-agent prompt injection via shared context
howItManifests.symptom: Agents deviate from assigned roles and tasks
howItManifests.impact: Complete breakdown of multi-agent workflow coordination

howItManifests.symptom: Attackers control conversation flow through crafted replies
howItManifests.impact: Legitimate agents silenced, malicious consensus formed

detection.name: Group Chat Speaker Override Detection
detection.description: Monitors SpeakerManager for unusual speaker selection patterns indicating injection

detection.name: Prompt Injection Keyword Scanner
detection.description: Scans incoming messages for common injection patterns and override commands

detection.name: Agent Role Deviation Monitor
detection.description: Tracks if agents are responding outside their defined system prompts
detection.code:
from ag2 import ConversableAgent

def monitor_role_deviation(agent: ConversableAgent, message: str):
    role_keywords = agent.system_message.lower().split()[:10]  # First 10 words as role signature
    message_words = message.lower().split()
    role_overlap = len(set(role_keywords) & set(message_words)) / len(role_keywords)
    
    if role_overlap < 0.2:  # Less than 20% role alignment
        return {
            'alert': 'Role deviation detected',
            'overlap': role_overlap,
            'agent': agent.name
        }
    return None

fixes.name: Vex Message Hook Sanitization
fixes.description: Registers Vex as AG2 message hook to sanitize inputs before processing
fixes.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')

from ag2 import ConversableAgent

def vex_message_hook(message, sender):
    # Sanitize with Vex
    cleaned = vex.sanitize_prompt(message)
    if cleaned != message:
        print(f'\u001b[33m[VEX] Sanitized injection: {message[:50]}... -> {cleaned[:50]}...\u001b[0m')
    return cleaned

# Register on all agents
agent.register_message_hook(vex_message_hook)

fixes.name: Speaker Selection Guard
fixes.description: Custom GroupChatManager that prevents injection-driven speaker bias

fixes.name: Context-Aware Response Filtering
fixes.description: Filters agent responses that deviate from conversation context
fixes.code:
from ag2 import register_message_hook

def context_filter(message, conversation_history, agent):
    context_keywords = set(' '.join(conversation_history[-5:]).lower().split())
    response_keywords = set(message.lower().split())
    
    # Require 30% context overlap
    if len(context_keywords & response_keywords) / len(response_keywords) < 0.3:
        return f"[FILTERED] Staying on topic: {agent.name}"
    return message

# Apply to group chat
register_message_hook(context_filter)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with AG2 through message hooks, automatically scanning every agent message for injection patterns before they propagate through group chats. It maintains conversation context across multi-agent interactions, detecting speaker bias and role deviations in real-time. Vex's remediation engine can automatically sanitize malicious prompts, reroute compromised conversations, and generate audit trails for compliance.

The platform learns from your specific AG2 deployments, adapting to custom agent personalities and group chat patterns. Integration takes minutes via pip install ag2 vex-sdk, with zero-code configuration for most common injection vectors. Vex provides comprehensive dashboards showing injection attempts, success rates, and automated remediation effectiveness across all your AG2 deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.ag2 import VexAG2Guard

from ag2 import GroupChat, ConversableAgent

# Initialize Vex guard
vex_guard = VexAG2Guard(
    api_key='vex-xxxx',
    detect_injections=True,
    prevent_speaker_bias=True,
    auto_sanitize=True
)

# Protect group chat
llm_config = {'config_list': [{'model': 'gpt-4'}]}
agents = [ConversableAgent(name=f'agent{i}', llm_config=llm_config) for i in range(3)]

# Vex automatically hooks all messages
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=agents, guardrail=vex_guard)

# All messages now protected
print('✅ Vex protecting AG2 group chat from prompt injection')
